header_subtitle: Mechanical Design Engineer passionate about ecological transition, responsible design, and new mobility solutions.

about.title: About Me
about.description: I’m Adrien Salicis, a mechanical design engineer trained at Polytech Lille. I have solid experience in mechanical design, manufacturing, industrial methods, and numerical modeling, developed through internships at Ciel et Terre, Getec, and Thales. My projects include the design of a low-tech velomobile, a robotic arm, and innovative cable-based transport systems. I'm passionate about outdoor activities, sports, and committed to ecological and technological transitions.

project_winddatas_desc: Python project developed during my final internship at Ciel & Terre. It collects, processes, and compares wind data from different sources (NOAA, Meteostat, OpenMeteo).
project_robotarm_desc: Mechatronics project carried out at Polytech Nice. Design and 3D printing of an articulated arm controlled by Arduino.
project_cabletransport_desc: Design of a system to transport loads via cable. Teamwork, CAD modeling, machining, waterjet cutting, mechanical assembly and testing.
project_velomobile_desc: Mechanical design project in a low-tech approach. Creation of a recumbent tandem tricycle modeled in Catia.
project_lowtech_workshops_desc: Co-led introductory workshops on the low-tech approach with a wide audience.
edu_cielterre_desc: Study of the sizing of floating solar plants. Development of internal tools.
edu_getec_desc: Welding, painting and fine sheet metal work in the railway industry. Industrialization and quality control.
edu_thales_desc: 3D printing for cable supports on an underwater drone. Creo modeling.
edu_uvigo_desc: Industrial design, FEM, combustion engines, laser technologies.
edu_polytech_desc: Design, CAD/CAM, stress analysis, heat transfer, eco-design.
